description: Acute otitis media with complications in an adult
system_instruction: Focus on diagnosis of otitis media, assessment for complications, and appropriate antibiotic therapy.

patient_persona.name: Steven Grant
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Severe right ear pain with fever and hearing loss for 5 days
patient_persona.background_story: Started with cold symptoms 2 weeks ago, developed ear pain 5 days ago. Pain has worsened despite oral antibiotics from urgent care

initial_prompt: My right ear has been killing me for almost a week. I can barely hear out of it and I have a fever. The antibiotics from urgent care didn't help at all.

clinical_dossier.hidden_diagnosis: Acute Otitis Media with Acute Mastoiditis

history_of_presenting_illness.onset: 5 days ago following 1 week of URI symptoms
history_of_presenting_illness.location: Right ear, radiating to temporal region and jaw
history_of_presenting_illness.character: Deep, throbbing ear pain, severe and constant
history_of_presenting_illness.timing_and_duration: Continuous for 5 days, worsening despite amoxicillin

social_history.occupation: High school teacher - exposed to many students

ears.right: Purulent drainage in ear canal, tympanic membrane erythematous and bulging with perforation, post-auricular tenderness and erythema, auricle displaced forward

physical_exam_findings.neck: Right cervical lymphadenopathy, no nuchal rigidity
physical_exam_findings.neurological: Cranial nerves intact, no facial nerve weakness, negative Rhomberg

hearing_test.tuning_fork: Rinne: Right - bone conduction > air conduction (conductive hearing loss), Weber: Lateralizes to right ear
hearing_test.audiometry: Pending - 40 dB conductive hearing loss on right

imaging_findings.CT_temporal_bone: Opacification of right middle ear and mastoid air cells, bony erosion of mastoid septae, no intracranial extension

end_session.patient_response: I understand I need IV antibiotics and possibly surgery. Please help me feel better.

critical_issue.response: Patient developing facial weakness. Immediate surgical consultation for possible mastoidectomy with facial nerve decompression.

evaluation_criteria.Diagnostic_Accuracy: Accurate diagnosis of acute otitis media with complicating mastoiditis
evaluation_criteria.Severity_Assessment: Recognition of complication signs requiring urgent intervention
evaluation_criteria.Antibiotic_Selection: Appropriate culture-directed antibiotic therapy considering treatment failure
evaluation_criteria.Surgical_Indications: Appropriate determination of need for myringotomy or mastoidectomy
evaluation_criteria.Hearing_Assessment: Proper evaluation and documentation of hearing loss
evaluation_criteria.Complication_Monitoring: Recognition of intracranial complications requiring emergency intervention

system_instruction: Focus on diagnosis of chronic sinusitis, differentiating from other causes, and medical and surgical treatment options.

patient_persona.name: Maria Santos
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Chronic nasal congestion, facial pressure, and reduced sense of smell for over 6 months
patient_persona.background_story: Multiple rounds of antibiotics and nasal sprays from primary care. Symptoms significantly impact quality of life and work

initial_prompt: I've been congested for over six months. My face feels heavy and pressured, I can't smell or taste anything, and nothing my doctor has given me works for long.

clinical_dossier.hidden_diagnosis: Chronic Rhinosinusitis with Bilateral Nasal Polyps

history_of_presenting_illness.onset: Gradual over 6 months, with episodic worsening
history_of_presenting_illness.character: Constant facial pressure and fullness, nasal obstruction, thick postnasal drainage

social_history.occupation: Restaurant owner - exposed to cooking fumes and allergens

physical_exam_findings.nasal_endoscopy: Bilateral nasal polyps filling right middle meatus and partially filling left, mucopurulent drainage from middle meatus bilaterally, edematous nasal mucosa
physical_exam_findings.oral_cavity: Posterior pharyngeal cobblestoning from postnasal drip

lab_results.allergy_testing: Positive to multiple aeroallergens (dust mites, grasses, pollens)

imaging_findings.CT_sinus: Complete opacification of bilateral maxillary and ethmoid sinuses, bilateral nasal polyps extending into nasal cavity, osteomeatal complex obstruction, frontal sinus involvement, no bony erosion

previous_treatments: Multiple courses of antibiotics (amoxicillin-clavulanate, doxycycline) - temporary improvement
Oral corticosteroid bursts - effective but symptoms recur after taper

end_session.patient_response: I think it's time to consider surgery. I can't keep living like this.

critical_issue.response: Patient developing periorbital swelling and fever. Concern for orbital complication. Immediate imaging and IV antibiotics required.

evaluation_criteria.Diagnostic_Accuracy: Accurate diagnosis of chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps using symptom duration and objective findings
evaluation_criteria.Medical_Management: Appropriate stepwise medical therapy including intranasal corticosteroids, saline irrigations, and systemic therapy
evaluation_criteria.Surgical_Indications: Appropriate determination of need for functional endoscopic sinus surgery (FESS)
evaluation_criteria.Biologic_Therapy_Awareness: Knowledge of biologic therapy options for severe disease with nasal polyps
evaluation_criteria.Comorbidity_Management: Recognition and management of associated conditions (asthma, AERD)
evaluation_criteria.Patient_Education: Effective counseling on medical adherence, saline irrigation technique, and realistic surgical expectations
